questions.text: Felix's hobbies include jogging, swimming, and ________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'climb mountains' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence.

questions.text: Mr. Delos Reyes requests that someone _________ the data by fax immediately.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'sends' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of a request.

questions.text: The concert will begin ________ fifteen minutes.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'in' because it is the most grammatically correct option to indicate a time frame.

questions.text: You were ____________ the Cebu office before 2 p.m.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'supposed to call' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence.

questions.text: I have only a ________ Christmas cards left to write.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'less' because it is used for uncountable nouns, but in this context, 'few' or 'fewer' could also be considered correct depending on the intended meaning.

questions.text: Each of the Olympic athletes ____________ for months, even years.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'have been training' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of multiple athletes.

questions.text: When I graduate from high school next March, I ______________ a student here for four years.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'will have been' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of future perfect tense.

questions.text: Mrs. Chun _________ rather not invest that money in the stock market.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'would' because it is the most grammatically correct option to express preference.

questions.text: No sooner ________ I shut the door, when the telephone rang.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'had' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of past perfect tense.

questions.text: ______ any one object if I turn the air-conditioner down?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Do' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of a question.

questions.text: An eagle, if pressed for food, might ______ a small baby that had been left in the open unprotected, but such an opportunity must occur very rarely.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'carry off' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of taking something away.

questions.text: Middle Eastern restaurants often have a way of whisking you away to a far-off land of belly dancers, exotic spices, and _______ drink.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'intoxicating' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing a type of drink.

questions.text: The little old lady down the street was taken ______ by a scam artist. He got more than $5000 from her!
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'in' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of being deceived.

questions.text: It is already 3 o'clock. Can you _____ time to catch the bus?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'make it in' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of arriving on time.

questions.text: Ireland was part of the UK, _____?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'wasn't it' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of a question tag.

questions.text: The New York police were very anxious _____ about the crime.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'to learn more' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of wanting more information.

questions.text: The sink in the locker room tends to _____.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'overflow' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing a common issue.

questions.text: We saw _____ wild animals while on vacation.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'quite a few' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing a quantity.

questions.text: Gene's house is _____ the hair salon. Do you think you can find it?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'next to' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing a location.

questions.text: The ski resorts are usually crowded. There are many people _____ skiing.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'who enjoy' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing people's activities.

questions.text: Colleen swimmmed one hundred laps in the Rizal Olympic pool yesterday.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'swam' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of past tense.

questions.text: Mr. Olsen is telephoning a American Red Cross for help.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'an' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of using an indefinite article before a noun starting with a vowel sound.

questions.text: Jean-Pierre will spend his long-awaited vacation either in Singapore nor the Bahamas.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'or' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of presenting alternatives.

questions.text: When we have reached our vacation, we plan to spend three days scuba diving down the reef.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'on' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing a location or a period of time, however 'in' could also be considered correct depending on the intended meaning.

questions.text: Osmond was expressing his reluctant when the other boys asked him to join them tomorrow.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'reluctance' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing a feeling or attitude.

questions.text: As usual, Roxanne was slow in expressing the opinion on the matter of the RH Bill
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'on' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing a topic or subject.

questions.text: We were fortunate enough to visit the Grand Canyon. It has much beautiful sceneries
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'many' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing countable nouns.

questions.text: Contrary to popular belief, a rabbit is not a kind of rodent; rather; it is a different species.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'but' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of presenting a contrast.

questions.text: Neither the children nor Teacher Sophia wish to go to the Clown party in the afternoon.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'wish' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of describing a plural subject.

questions.text: If it were for the shortcomings of the administration, the team would have won the award.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'were' because it is the most grammatically correct option to complete the sentence in the context of a conditional sentence.
